summary.intent: Fix cross-machine session portability in Gossamer: sessions saved on one computer should resume correctly on another where the repo lives at a different absolute path. Also fix related UI issues with stale timestamps, missing checkpoint data, and incorrect database path defaults.
summary.outcome: Fixed session resume to use current workspace root instead of stored cwd, fixed checkpoint tree provider to use server-side repoRoot, fixed shadow indexer and session API to skip permission-mode events when extracting cwd/branch/timestamp, fixed session list timestamps via raw SQL, and fixed the default DB path to be per-repo instead of a shared hook-handler.db.

repo: Claude CLI now emits a `permission-mode` event as the very first JSONL event — it has a sessionId but no cwd, gitBranch, or timestamp. Any code that finds the 'first event' by id or by `e.sessionId || e.cwd || e.gitBranch` will incorrectly match this no-op event.
Session API returns `repoRoot` (set from server's repoDir) which is the reliable cross-machine path, while `cwd` comes from LogEvents and can be null/empty due to permission-mode events.
Both repos (cosmos-graphql and gossamer) were sharing the same hook-handler.db because serve.ts defaulted to ~/.claude/hook-handler.db and POST /api/repos inherited that path into config.
defaultDbPath() in src/config.ts generates ~/.gossamer/repos/<name>.db with unsafe chars replaced by underscores.
CheckpointTreeProvider skips branch-log fetch entirely when sessionInfo.cwd is falsy — so null cwd from the permission-mode bug caused the entire Checkpoints sidebar to be empty.
Prisma 7 groupBy._max on DateTime with SQLite can return null/incorrect values due to TEXT storage format — raw SQL queryRawUnsafe is needed for reliable timestamp aggregation.
The cutoff filter in CheckpointTreeProvider uses updatedAt to exclude post-session checkpoints — when updatedAt falls back to epoch (1970), every checkpoint gets filtered out.

code.finding: events.find((e) => e.sessionId || e.cwd || e.gitBranch) matched permission-mode events (which have sessionId) before finding events with actual cwd/gitBranch. Fixed to search for each field independently.
findFirst orderBy: {id: 'asc'} returned permission-mode event (null cwd). Fixed with separate queries filtering for non-null cwd and non-null timestamp.

Prisma groupBy._max for DateTime unreliable on SQLite. Replaced with raw SQL: SELECT sessionId, MIN(timestamp) AS startedAt, MAX(timestamp) AS updatedAt FROM LogEvent WHERE sessionId IN (?) AND timestamp IS NOT NULL GROUP BY sessionId
currentRepoPath was set from sessionInfo.cwd (stored path, possibly from another machine). Fixed to use sessionInfo.repoRoot with cwd as fallback.
Branch-log API call passed sessionInfo.cwd as localPath — findRepoForPath() would fail if path doesn't match current machine. Fixed to use repoRoot.

Cutoff filter uses updatedAt. When updatedAt is epoch (due to timestamp bug), cutoff becomes ~1970 and all checkpoints are filtered out. Fixed by skipping filter when updatedAt < 1_000_000_000_000 ms (pre-Sept 2001).
Terminal cwd for resume used msg.cwd (stored path). Fixed to use vscode.workspace.workspaceFolders[0].uri.fsPath with stored cwd as fallback.
Resume endpoint used session's stored cwd. Fixed to use repoDir (server's known local path) with fallback chain: repoDir ?? cwd ?? HOME ?? /tmp
Hardcoded default DB path was ~/.claude/hook-handler.db (shared across all repos). Fixed to use defaultDbPath(basename(repoDir)) for per-repo isolation.
POST /api/repos inherited server's dbPath when registering new repos, permanently baking hook-handler.db into config. Fixed to always use defaultDbPath(name) for new repos.

workflow: Edits to files were silently lost multiple times during the session — the assistant had to re-apply several changes after discovering grep showed them missing. This suggests a file-system conflict or the editor reloading files from disk.
The built extension in dist/ was stale (last built Apr 7 22:22) — running extension used old code, making it hard to distinguish bugs from unbuilt changes.
When APIs return correct data but the UI shows nothing, check client-side guard conditions (truthy checks on empty strings, cutoff filters) before assuming the server is broken.

friction: Multiple edits were silently lost during the session — the assistant had to rediscover and re-apply changes to SessionDetailPanel.ts, server.ts (raw SQL fix), CheckpointTreeProvider.ts, and the resume endpoint at least once each.
The running VS Code extension was using a stale dist/ build, making it impossible to test fixes without a rebuild step that was never completed.
Debugging was complicated by three separate root causes (stored cwd, permission-mode event, shared DB) producing similar symptoms (empty UI, wrong paths).
The Prisma groupBy._max SQLite DateTime serialization issue is a silent failure — it returns null instead of erroring, causing fallback to session start time.
open_items: Existing repos in ~/.gossamer/config.json that still point to hook-handler.db need manual migration — update config and re-index to give each repo its own DB.
The built VS Code extension (dist/) was never rebuilt with the fixes — user needs to run a build step before changes take effect in the running extension.
Shadow sessions with null cwd/gitBranch already stored in the DB won't be retroactively fixed — a re-index of existing sessions may be needed.

The `repoRoot` field on the session API response is set from server's repoDir — if the server is started without a known repo (edge case), repoRoot could still be null and the fallback to cwd would trigger the old bug.
No migration path documented for users with multiple repos sharing hook-handler.db to split them into per-repo databases.
